question: What is the definition of the abbreviation RRV?

nuscale_ch01_0064.title: NuScale Test and Inspection Plans
nuscale_ch01_0064.summary: This section describes NuScale’s test and inspection plans for key reactor components, including control rod drive shafts and Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS) valves. It summarizes test results confirming operability and performance under various conditions, and outlines a dedicated test program—conducted at a specialized facility—to demonstrate ECCS valve functionality at reactor conditions, including unique design features like the inadvertent actuation block (IAB).

nuscale_ch01_0063.title: Emergency Core Cooling System Valve Design Certification Application Demonstration Test
nuscale_ch01_0063.summary: This section describes the demonstration test program for the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS) valves in the NuScale reactor design. The program aimed to verify the functional performance of the ECCS valves, including their operation at reactor pressures and temperatures and in borated coolant, as well as to test unique features like the inadvertent actuation block (IAB). Testing was conducted at a specialized facility using a test article representative of the reactor recirculation valve (RRV) and included all key valve components.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis diagrams. It defines the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system status and operations, ensuring clear interpretation of the diagrams. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, detailing the standardized symbols and notations for various instruments, probes, flow meters, and accessory devices. It includes representations for sample probes, pressure and temperature sensors, flow measurement devices, and level indicators, ensuring consistent interpretation of P&ID drawings throughout the documentation.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in compliance with regulatory requirements.

edges.description: Section A and Section B are identical excerpts, both mentioning that RVV stands for reactor vent valves, thus Section A specifies the definition of the abbreviation RRV (likely a typographical error for RVV) as used in Section B.

Section A mentions reactor vent valves (RVVs) and reactor recirculation valves (RRVs), while Section B provides diagram legends that likely define abbreviations such as RRV, connecting the abbreviation's use in A to its definition in B.

Section A mentions reactor vent valves (RVVs) as part of the ECCS, while Section B provides diagram legends that likely define abbreviations such as RRV, connecting the abbreviation's use in A to its definition in B.

Section A mentions 'reactor vent valves (RVVs)' as part of the ECCS, while Section B is an acronyms and abbreviations section that would define such abbreviations, connecting the use of 'RVV' in A to its definition in B.
Section B contains the list of acronyms and abbreviations, which is where the definition of 'RRV' would be found, while Section A provides diagram legends but does not define abbreviations.
